metadata.method: Inline simulation: Claude read current SKILL.md and graded what the skill would produce for each prompt. Not independent subagent execution.
metadata.note: Eval 5 (non-interactive-eof-fix) is the skill's core strength. Evals 1-4 cover skill-creation workflows the current skill does not address.

notes: Eval 5 (non-interactive EOF fix) is a perfect score — this is the skill's entire purpose.
Evals 1-4 are all near-zero because skill-making has no skill-creation workflow, no naming rules, no review checklist, and no triggering guidance.
Eval 2 gets 1/6 only because the skill's own frontmatter template happens to include metadata fields, giving indirect awareness.
Eval 4 gets 2/6: SKILL.md is created (correct), scripts/ placement is correct — everything else (kebab-case, no CHANGELOG, no frontmatter, README.md violation) fails.
The merged skill-creator must score >= 1.0 on eval 5 to be accepted — do not degrade the non-interactive patterns.
Target: >= 0.75 overall mean pass rate to justify the rewrite.
